Description: JWT bearer validation disabled in emulator accepts arbitrary tokens
Justification: TokenValidationParameters disables issuer, audience, lifetime, and signing key validation, effectively accepting any token presented via Authorization header or access_token query.
ImpactSummary: Allows unauthenticated users to obtain authorized access to SignalR emulator endpoints with arbitrary identities and roles.

Description: Hardcoded symmetric JWT secret in emulator source code
Justification: AccessKey constant is embedded in code and used to derive ValidKeys; leaked in console output, enabling token forging if emulator is exposed.
InjectionVector: Forge JWT with HS256 using leaked AccessKey
ImpactSummary: Allows forging of tokens and impersonation within emulator scope.

Description: Emulator JWT auth disables all validation (issuer, audience, lifetime, signing key) enabling token acceptance without verification
Justification: TokenValidationParameters sets ValidateIssuer, ValidateAudience, ValidateLifetime, and ValidateIssuerSigningKey to false, allowing arbitrary tokens (including via access_token query) to be treated as valid.
ImpactSummary: Authentication bypass within emulator: arbitrary clients can connect and assume any identity/claims because signature, issuer, audience, and lifetime validation are disabled.

Description: JWT bearer validation disabled in emulator accepts arbitrary tokens
Justification: TokenValidationParameters disables issuer, audience, lifetime, and signing key validation, effectively accepting any token presented via Authorization header or access_token query.
InjectionVector: Authorization header (Bearer) or query parameter: access_token
ImpactSummary: Authentication bypass within the emulator: attackers can self-assign identities/claims and access [Authorize]-protected emulator endpoints or hubs.

Description: Hardcoded symmetric JWT secret in emulator source code
Justification: AccessKey constant is embedded in code and used to derive ValidKeys; leaked in console output, enabling token forging if emulator is exposed.
InjectionVector: HTTP Authorization: Bearer <forged HS256 token> or access_token query parameter; AsrsSignature header for upstream spoofing.
ImpactSummary: Known symmetric key enables forging of authentication tokens and upstream signatures, allowing impersonation and potential full access when the emulator is exposed.

VulnerabilitySubType: Detailed error messages in HTTP response
Description: Exception message written directly to HTTP response in negotiate flow
Justification: ServiceRouteHelper catches exceptions and writes ex.Message/e.Message to the HTTP response, leaking internal details to unauthenticated clients.
InjectionVector: HTTP request to negotiate endpoint; response body contains exception message text.
ImpactSummary: Returns internal exception messages to clients, revealing service state and internal error text. This can aid reconnaissance and targeted exploitation.

Description: Unity editor inputs concatenated into ProcessStartInfo.Arguments and executed via Process.Start
Justification: Editor TextField values flow into MpcArgument.ToString() and are assigned to ProcessStartInfo.Arguments without validation or robust quoting, enabling argument injection into the mpc CLI.
InjectionVector: Unity Editor text fields for MpcArgument properties (e.g., Input, Output, ConditionalSymbol, ResolverName, Namespace, MultipleIfDirectiveOutputSymbols).
ImpactSummary: Allows manipulation of mpc invocation by injecting additional CLI flags/values through unquoted fields, potentially overwriting files or generating code in attacker-controlled locations within the developer machine.
